content: A 2000 study found that _Lactobacillus reuteri_ supplementation before a cholesterol-raising diet reduced the need for parenteral nutrition and hospitalization by 11 days compared to non-fat milk supplementation.
_Lactobacillus reuteri_ supplementation in preterm infants reduced the risk of sepsis by 66% and mortality by 79%, according to a 2016 meta-analysis.
A 2018 meta-analysis found that _Lactobacillus reuteri_ supplementation reduced time spent crying or fussing in breastfed infants with colic compared to placebo.
_Lactobacillus reuteri_ is an interesting microbe with diverse effects, including strengthening the gut barrier and immune system, improving metabolism, and protecting against digestive diseases.
There are no established clinical dosage recommendations for _Lactobacillus reuteri_ or most other probiotics, as research into the use of probiotics is still relatively new.
